name: Traeger Pro 575 Wi-Fi Pellet Grill
whatWorks: Industry-leading WiFIRE app is genuinely reliable — monitors temps, sends alerts, and lets you adjust or shut down the grill from your phone without the connection drops common on competitor apps.
D2 PID controller holds temperature within ~5°F at low-and-slow settings (225–250°F), producing consistently tender ribs, brisket, and pulled pork with a clean smoke ring.
TurboTemp helps the grill recover quickly after lid lifts and keeps temps stable during cold Canadian winters — no insulated blanket needed for most fall/winter cooks.
Porcelain-coated grates show no rust after years of use and are easy to clean — a practical advantage over bare cast iron in Canada's wet shoulder seasons.
worthKnowing: The Pro 575 was discontinued by Traeger in early 2025 and replaced by the Traeger Woodridge, which offers more cooking space, better ash cleanup, and a 10-year warranty for roughly the same price — only worth buying the Pro 575 if found at a notable discount.
Smoke flavor is noticeably subtler than charcoal or offset smokers; even at 225°F, pellet smoke is mild. Running overnight at 180°F before bumping to 250°F is the common workaround for deeper flavor.
No direct flame searing option — the 500°F max through indirect heat can't match a gas grill for steaks. If you want one outdoor cooker to do both smoking and searing, this isn't it.

The 18 lb hopper holds less than a standard 20 lb pellet bag, meaning you'll have 2 lb of leftovers every refill. The side-mounted grease tin can also tip over — worth lining with foil and checking before each cook.
verdict: The Traeger Pro 575 is a genuinely great beginner smoker that makes low-and-slow BBQ approachable: reliable app, accurate temps, and tender results without babysitting. However, Traeger discontinued it in 2025 and its replacement (the Woodridge) offers meaningfully more at the same price — so the Pro 575 is only a smart buy at clearance. If you find it under $500 CAD, it's excellent value for someone who wants set-and-forget smoking without complexity.

realBuyers.quote: I've had mine since 2020 and use it almost every week — the app is legitimately the best part, being able to bump the temp from bed at midnight without going outside is a game changer.
The smoke flavor is subtle compared to my old Weber Smokey Mountain, but honestly my family prefers it — not everything needs to taste like a campfire.
Great grill but just know it can't replace your propane grill for steaks — it's a smoker first and a grill second.

metaDescription: Traeger Pro 575 review for Canadian buyers: excellent WiFIRE app, consistent low-and-slow temps, and beginner-friendly operation. Best value at clearance prices.
introText: The Traeger Pro 575 is the entry-level model in Traeger's WiFi-enabled pellet grill lineup, featuring a PID D2 controller, 572 sq in of cooking space, and the well-regarded WiFIRE app for remote monitoring. It was Traeger's most popular smoker until the Woodridge replaced it in early 2025, but it remains available at $600 CAD on Amazon.ca and offers solid set-and-forget performance for anyone new to pellet smoking.
